title: Deep operator attention module
goal: Extract a workflow::attention module that owns operator-attention delivery and dedupe semantics for worker questions, replan decisions, terminal transitions, and cockpit pane hygiene, so daemon and manager callers only emit typed attention intents.
acceptance: All existing projection snapshots, parity fixtures, and golden output tests remain behavior-preserving unless an intentional divergence is explicitly documented in this slice output.
A workflow::attention module exposes a small typed interface for worker-question pending, replan-decision pending, terminal transition notification, and worker-pane terminal rename intents.

Origin target lookup, payload schema versioning, Herdr send/focus/rename calls through the cockpit seam, transition-keyed terminal dedupe, and non-fatal visibility incident recording live behind the attention module interface.

daemon.rs no longer has its own inline worker-question Herdr origin lookup/send/focus implementation; workflow/manager.rs no longer has separate replan/terminal/pane attention delivery logic duplicated by kind.
Attention failures remain warning/visibility incidents only and do not affect run status, slice status, question/proposal state, verification, merge, handoff readiness, or final SHA.
Tests cover no-origin no-op, delivery failure incident, dedupe by concrete attention key/terminal transition, and both worker-question and replan attention paths through the shared module.
must_ask_if: The implementation would auto-answer a worker question, auto-accept/reject/defer a proposal, or make a notification authoritative.
The implementation would introduce a second Herdr/Pi delivery path instead of the cockpit seam and origin target artifact.
The implementation would require Herdr for headless status, answer, replan, handoff, or completion.
The implementation would remove durable terminal notification records or broaden dedupe so later resumed transitions cannot notify.
